_readme.what: Faithful export of CastBot's Discord menus for recreating them in a React Discord-prototyping webapp. Payloads below are the EXACT JSON structures CastBot sends to Discord's interaction API (Components V2), captured from the real builder functions (app.js createProductionMenuInterface, playerManagement.js createPlayerManagementUI).
_readme.format: Discord Components V2. Every menu is a message with flags including 1<<15 (IS_COMPONENTS_V2) and usually 1<<6 (EPHEMERAL). The message body is a single 'Container' component (type 17) holding everything.
howToRender: Render each menu as a Discord message card: dark chat background, with the Container drawn as a rounded box with a 4px left accent bar colored `accent_color` (integer RGB, e.g. 3447003 = #3498DB).
Walk `components` in order, top to bottom. Each entry is one block.

type 10 (Text Display): render `content` as Discord markdown. '## ' = large heading, '### ' = small heading, '-# ' = tiny grey caption text, '> ' = blockquote, **bold**, and triple-backtick spans render as inline code blocks (CastBot uses '### ```📍 Section Name```' as its standard section-heading style — visually a small heading containing a code-block pill).
type 14 (Separator): thin horizontal divider line with vertical padding.
type 1 (Action Row): horizontal flex row of up to 5 buttons OR exactly 1 select menu.

type 2 (Button): styles → 1 Primary (blurple #5865F2), 2 Secondary (grey #4E5058), 3 Success (green #248046), 4 Danger (red #DA373C), 5 Link. `emoji.name` is a unicode emoji rendered left of `label`. `disabled: true` = 50% opacity, not clickable.
type 3 (String Select): full-width dropdown with `placeholder`; options have label/value/description/emoji.
type 5 (User Select): full-width dropdown that picks a server member; render like a string select with a placeholder.

type 9 (Section): a row with `components` (1-3 Text Displays) on the LEFT and a single `accessory` on the RIGHT. Accessory is either a Button (type 2) or a Thumbnail (type 11, renders `media.url` as a ~80px rounded-corner image).
Custom Discord emoji like '<:castbot_logo:123...>' can't render outside Discord — substitute the provided `fallback` emoji.
Active-tab pattern: in nav rows, the currently-active button is style 1 (Primary/blurple) and the rest are style 2 (grey). Active buttons stay clickable, never disabled.

production_menu.name: Production Menu
production_menu.notes: The main admin hub. The 'Post Castlists' row is dynamic per-server: always starts with Post Castlist + Compact Castlist, then up to 3 custom castlist buttons (grey, custom emoji). Sample custom castlists included below. The Setup button is RED (style 4) until server onboarding is complete, then grey (style 2).

components.content: > **📅 Season Apps** has been moved to Season Manager below

components.label: Setup
components._note: style 4 (red) until server setup complete, then style 2 (grey)

player_menu.name: Player Menu
player_menu.trigger: /menu slash command (non-admin users), or 'Player Menu' accessory button on Production Menu
player_menu.notes: Player-facing self-service menu. Buttons in the section rows are conditional: Safari row (currency/inventory/map/etc.) only shows once the player has Safari activity; this payload shows a fully-unlocked player. Clicking a category button turns it blurple (style 1 = active) and swaps the select menu at the bottom ('hot-swap select') to that category's options — the select shown here is the Pronouns select as an example. The player card is a Section with the user's avatar as a Thumbnail accessory.

components.label: Pronouns
components._note: style 1 because 'pronouns' is the active category in this example

components.label: Coins
components._note: label/emoji are per-server custom terms; defaults: Currency 🪙
This is the hot-swap select — its options change based on which category button is active (pronouns shown here). For Timezone it lists timezone roles, for Castlists it lists castlists, etc.

player_manager_admin.name: Player Manager (admin variant of Player Menu)
player_manager_admin.notes: Same layout engine as player_menu with three differences: (1) a User Select at the top to pick which player to manage, (2) buttons use admin_set_* custom_ids and are DISABLED until a player is selected, (3) extra buttons: 'Vanity Roles' 🎭 in the profile row, 'Stamina' ⚡ in the Safari row, and a '⬅ Menu' back button in the footer next to Logs.

userSelectRow._position: directly under the title, after a separator

extraButtons.row: 🦁 Safari (4th button, between Safari Map and Challenges)

setup_wizard.name: Setup Wizard (first-run replacement of Production Menu)
setup_wizard.trigger: Shown INSTEAD of production_menu when a server hasn't completed setup
setup_wizard.notes: Onboarding checklist card: welcome text plus step buttons that turn into green done-ticks as steps complete (setup → create castlist → post castlist → create season). Not exported in full — ask for a follow-up dump if needed.

navigation._note: What each Production Menu button opens, so the prototype can wire click-through navigation.
navigation.show_castlist2_default: Posts the castlist display (public message with player cards) — separate screen
navigation.castlist_hub_main: Castlist Manager submenu (create/edit castlists, sort strategy, placements)
navigation.season_manager: Season Manager submenu — active-tab nav row: 📝 Season Apps | 🗓️ Planner | 🏆 Casting | ✏️ Edit
